recommendations: add a minimal smoke-test suite for the MCP plugin (import scipy

`csr_matrix`/`gaussian_kde` duplicates

`OptimizeWarning`
`ValueError`) into structured MCP error payloads
introduce per-endpoint input validation schemas (Pydantic) for array-like args
dtypes
shapes
and bounds to fail fast before deep SciPy calls

large eigensolvers) with configurable limits
implement deterministic-seed support for stochastic APIs (`differential_evolution`

add observability hooks (structured logs

add security hardening for deserialization and input size caps to prevent memory abuse

add performance benchmarks for high-traffic endpoints and regressions thresholds

add end-to-end examples for common MCP workflows (solve linear system
run optimizer
compute FFT
statistical test) and expected JSON I/O
implement semantic versioning for the plugin API and changelog entries for endpoint additions/removals
add golden tests for complex return-object serialization (`OptimizeResult`
sparse matrices
interpolation objects) to ensure stable wire formats

success_factors: End-to-end workflow completed with status=success across all nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Import-based adapter strategy is appropriate for SciPy (import feasibility 0.91, strategy confidence 0.94).
Plugin service booted healthy over stdio, indicating baseline MCP runtime viability.
Repository ingestion succeeded despite fallback path (zip_fallback), avoiding hard stop.
failure_reasons: Original project tests were not executed successfully (passed=false, test files empty, execution_time=0), so source-level validation is missing.
DeepWiki analysis failed, reducing external architecture/context enrichment.
Generated artifact metrics show zeros (LOC/files size/tool endpoints), indicating instrumentation/reporting gaps.

node_performance.download_time: Completed successfully; exact per-node timing unavailable. Zip fallback likely added overhead but remained within total 217.58s.
node_performance.analysis_time: Completed successfully on a very large codebase (~1336 files scanned); likely a major share of runtime.
node_performance.generation_time: Completed and produced expected MCP scaffold files; timing not broken out.
node_performance.test_time: MCP plugin health check passed quickly, but no meaningful functional/performance tests were run.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Unknown from telemetry (reported 0 MB); likely acceptable for scaffold generation but unverified for runtime endpoints.
